Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Article Title: What are gold, silver hallmarking charges? Check details and importance
Article Text:
What is gold, silver hallmarking?



Hallmarking charges



ALSO READ |

Testing for purity



The importance of hallmarking



Old hallmarking signs



ALSO READ |

Hallmark symbols



HUID in the hallmarked gold jewellery



Key components of hallmark on gold jewellery



Gold, silver hallmarking : Gold and silver jewllery have long been treasured for their beauty and value. However, ensuring their purity and authenticity is important, especially in a market prone to fraudulent practices. This is where the hallmarking scheme comes into play. Here’s what you need to know about hallmarking charges and its procedures:Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver.It serves as a guarantee of their purity or fineness. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820, according to an ET report.Hallmarking charges are applied per article, regardless of its weight. For gold articles, the charge is Rs 45 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 200 for a consignment. Similarly, for silver articles, the charge is Rs 35 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 150 for a consignment. It's important to note that additional taxes and levies may apply.A consignment refers to a collection of articles with the same fineness, from which a sample is drawn and assayed for compliance. Different lots may exist within a consignment.Anyone can have their hallmarked jewellery tested for purity at an A&H centre by paying a testing fee of Rs. 200 (for testing using the fire assay method as per IS 1418). These centres must be recognized by the BIS. The list of recognized A&H centres is available on the BIS website under the hallmarking section.The primary objective of hallmarking is to safeguard consumers from counterfeit products. To achieve this, the Indian government made hallmarking mandatory for gold jewellery from June 16, 2021. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification ( HUID ) number.If you bought gold jewellery on or before June 30, 2021, it may have old hallmarking signs. These signs include the purity/fineness mark and the BIS emblem, along with two additional indications: the jeweller's identification number and the assay centre's mark/number.However, starting July 1, 2021, these two indications were replaced with a unique alphanumeric HUID code. The hallmark remains valid for the lifetime of the jewellery, and consumers can sell old jewellery, whether hallmarked or un-hallmarked, to jewellers.The HUID, or hallmark Unique Identification number, is a key component of hallmarking in gold jewellery. It's a unique six-digit alphanumeric code assigned to each hallmarked item, allowing for traceability. Customers can verify the HUID number using the 'Verify HUID' feature in the BIS Care App.Since the introduction of the HUID on July 1, 2021, the hallmark on gold jewellery consists of three marks: the BIS logo, the purity of the article in caratage and fineness, and a unique six-digit alphanumeric HUID number.
Article Summary: Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification number.

Include property, investments, bank accounts, insurance policies, and any valuable items you own.Decide who will inherit your assets by choosing your beneficiaries. You can assign specific items to each beneficiary and select backup beneficiaries in case your first choices can't inherit. Then, choose an executor, the person who will carry out your will.Use one of the customizable templates available on the platform. Follow the instructions to fill in the details, specifying how you want your assets distributed and including any special conditions or terms.You can review the drafted will to ensure it's accurate and make changes if needed. If you have complicated issues, it's best to get legal advice. Once you're satisfied, you can legally execute the will according to the platform's rules, usually by electronically signing it with witnesses. The completed will is then stored securely online, with encryption for added security.With inputs from Centre for Investment Education and Learning content which appeared in Economic Times
Article Summary: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro data leak: Tata Electronics breach exposes key components — What we know
Article Text:
What has leaked:

An alleged ransomware attack on Tata Electronics has left Apple’s sensitive supply chain data exposed on the dark web. The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack.The exposure could impact Apple’s tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones. It may also put pressure on Apple’s relationship with Tata, which has emerged as a key manufacturing partner outside China. The leak could additionally offer rivals, counterfeiters and vendors rare insight into Apple’s supplier network.Tata Electronics, which both supplies parts and assembles iPhones, is a key part of Apple’s push to expand manufacturing outside China. This move also fits into India’s plan, supported by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, to grow into a major electronics manufacturing hub.Apple is expected to launch its iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max in September. The leak comes as the company faces higher costs, after recently raising prices of iPads and MacBooks due to rising chip costs.Analysts expect iPhone prices may also increase in the coming months.Reuters reported that more than 200,000 files from Tata Electronics were posted on the dark web by a group called World Leaks. Those files reportedly included design documents for older iPhones and some Tesla parts, both linked to Tata clients. Documents linked to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (2330.TW) and Qualcomm (QCOM.O), both key Apple suppliers, were also part of the leak.New documents reviewed by Reuters show that at least six files map parts of the iPhone 18 Pro models to specific suppliers. These include parts for the main circuit board, battery and camera systems.A source familiar with the matter said Apple treats this supplier information as highly sensitive, especially for unreleased products. The documents reportedly show supplier details that Apple does not publish publicly.Overall, the files cover hundreds of parts expected to be used in the iPhone 18 Pro models. They also show where Apple uses multiple suppliers for some parts and fewer suppliers for others, revealing both strengths and risks in its supply chain.World Leaks has previously claimed responsibility for a breach involving Nike. Reuters has not confirmed the authenticity of the data and could not reach the group for comment. AppleInsider first reported that iPhone 18 Pro documents were included in the Tata leak.Reuters has reported that Apple is investigating the breach and working with Tata on security steps. Tata has restricted access to internal systems and hired a global consultancy for a forensic audit.Some leaked files also carry Apple “confidential” marks and internal code names linked to the iPhone 18 Pro series, according to a source.The leak folder also includes photos of iPhones being drop-tested at a Tata facility in early 2026. The images show a grey phone with a triple rear camera setup and the Apple logo. The source said these are iPhone 18 Pro models, although Reuters could not confirm this.India is expected to make 26% of global iPhone production in 2026, up from 6% four years ago, according to Counterpoint Research.
Article Summary: The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack. The exposure could impact Apple's tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones.

Article Title: Researchers just found that job seekers may be better off replying fast, because an analysis of 11.6 million marketplace interactions suggests slower responses hurt hiring chances and quick replies ma
Article Text:
In today's job market, especially for freelancers, how quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Speed Is a Signal: When

Faster Replies

Increase Hiring Likelihood

The hidden psychology of instant

digital communication

New research reveals that employers form quick impressions based on reply times, often interpreting faster responses as enthusiasm and reliability. This study highlights that even small delays can cost you opportunities, making prompt communication a crucial skill for professional success in the modern workplace. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Getting an undeniable advantage in the contemporary hybrid workplace

When we look at the small anxieties built into modern technology, few things are as tense as the text bubble. We watch those three little dots dance on our screens, or we glance at our phones, hoping for a notification banner to break a long, quiet afternoon silence. In personal life, people often read response times as a sign of anything from busyness to distance. For a long time, we treated these digital habits as simple, casual quirks of our social lives. But new tracking data shows that even small delays can matter when you are trying to land a job.Imagine submitting a spectacular portfolio or matching perfectly with a freelance listing online, only to have the entire opportunity slip away simply because you decided to wait until after dinner to reply to an initial inquiry. In the modern, always-connected freelance market, your professional destiny is often decided long before you ever jump on an introductory phone call or sit down for a formal video panel. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply.This profound behavioural pattern was detailed in a research paper published in the journal Management Science titled. The study utilised transaction data from approximately eleven point six million real-world marketplace interactions on a massive global freelancing portal.The data showed that longer reply delays were linked to a lower chance of getting the contract, making response speed an important competitive factor.To comprehend how the quick message influences contemporary recruitment managers, one should consider the psychological factors that exist beyond the screen. When the client contacts the freelancer or a potential worker, they do not only assess their technical competence, past projects, or star rating. What they need is to find the intangible level of professional security and trust between both parties. According to the results obtained during several experiment stages, a quick answer could be interpreted as a sign of enthusiasm and hard work.The behavioural trials suggest that replying quickly can change how an evaluator judges a candidate's character. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts. It also signalled future responsiveness, making employers more likely to see the candidate as organised and reliable.This study reveals the possibility that the halo effect could operate regardless of the message content or past quality metrics. Sometimes, even if the slow responder received better reviews or had a lower price, he still managed to win the contract. Reading a late message is likely to be taken as a signal of indifference or lack of organisation, which makes it harder to contact the slower employee under deadlines.The practical insights gained from this large-scale market research data provide the key tool for all those who are working in the competitive world of freelance and remote recruitment. In the world of hybrid workforces and teams around the globe, being reachable becomes a valuable professional skill that can be used to enhance one’s market value without any additional certification.By setting up notifications, keeping templates ready, and replying promptly during hiring windows, independent professionals can signal professionalism to potential buyers. This proactive approach helps build an immediate sense of social comfort and trust with a potential client before any physical work even begins. It can turn a digital exchange into a more personal interaction and give fast responders an advantage over slower competitors.The results of the research suggest that reaction time could be regarded as a signal. Fast reactions of the respondents will help to reassure the client, establish one’s reputation and increase one’s opportunities of being hired. Workers may demonstrate their professionalism by fast reactions.
Article Summary: How quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts.

Article Title: Meet China's new AI humanoid robot built for homes with natural conversations and human-like expressions
Article Text:
China's UBTech introduces a humanoid companion for the home



Human-like design meets on-device artificial intelligence



How UBTech engineered more natural human expressions



Why home environments pose a greater robotics challenge



China's robotics companies are widening their ambitions



The idea of a humanoid robot in an ordinary home has often belonged to science fiction rather than everyday life. That distinction is beginning to blur as manufacturers move beyond warehouses and production lines in search of new customers. Chinese robotics companies have spent years refining machines for industrial work, but attention is slowly shifting towards products designed to share living spaces with people instead of factory floors. UBTech's latest release reflects that wider change. Rather than concentrating on lifting, sorting or repetitive labour, the company has built a full-sized humanoid intended for conversation, companionship and long-term interaction inside the home.The launch signals another stage in China's expanding robotics industry, where advances in artificial intelligence, hardware design and manufacturing are making increasingly sophisticated personal robots commercially available.Shenzhen-based UBTech has introduced the U1, a full-sized humanoid robot aimed at adults seeking an interactive companion rather than an industrial assistant. Unlike most existing humanoid machines, which are designed to complete repetitive tasks in controlled workplaces, the U1 has been developed with everyday household environments in mind.According to South China Morning Post (SCMP), the robot is available in both male and female versions, measuring 183cm and 168cm respectively. Buyers can choose between Lite, Pro and Ultra models, with prices beginning at 119,800 yuan and reaching as high as 990,000 yuan for the premium version.Instead of presenting itself as another productivity tool, the U1 focuses on communication. It has been designed to maintain eye contact during conversations, respond naturally to users and spend extended periods interacting with people inside domestic settings.A large part of the robot's identity comes from its appearance. UBTech has covered the U1 with silicone skin intended to create a more human-like exterior than the exposed mechanical designs commonly seen on industrial robots.SCMP reported, that inside the machine contains 88 servo joints that allow a broad range of movement. Its artificial intelligence runs locally on Rockchip's RK3588 processor, allowing many interactions to occur directly on the device rather than relying on remote cloud processing.The company has also emphasised privacy. User information remains stored on the robot itself rather than being routinely uploaded online, an approach that addresses growing concerns about personal data within connected home devices.Creating movement was only part of the challenge. According to UBTech executives, reproducing believable facial expressions required a far more complicated engineering process than simply fitting motors into a humanoid body.Thousands of individual components are housed inside the robot's head, working together with artificial intelligence models that synchronise facial movements with spoken language. The objective was to avoid the mismatch that often makes humanoid robots appear unnatural when speaking.Engineers also faced the difficulty of fitting these complex systems into proportions similar to those of a human while maintaining reliable performance across large-scale production rather than isolated prototypes.The consumer market presents a very different set of demands from industrial robotics. Factory robots typically operate in predictable surroundings where layouts rarely change and safety zones are carefully controlled.Homes are far less structured. Furniture is moved, pets wander through rooms and family members of different ages create constantly changing conditions. A domestic robot therefore has to navigate spaces that are far less predictable while interacting safely with people over long periods.Many of the world's leading humanoid robot developers continue to earn most of their business from industrial customers. Tesla's Optimus programme remains focused largely on manufacturing applications, while Figure AI has concentrated on warehouse work through enterprise partnerships.Chinese developers have followed a similar route. Firms including Unitree Robotics and AgiBot have prioritised industrial deployments while scaling up production capabilities.UBTech is attempting to maintain that industrial business while gradually opening a second market centred on consumers. The U1 represents one of the company's clearest moves towards personal robotics rather than workplace automation alone.
Article Summary: Shenzhen-based UBTech has introduced the U1, a full-sized humanoid robot aimed at adults seeking an interactive companion. The launch signals another stage in China's expanding robotics industry, where advances in artificial intelligence, hardware design and manufacturing are making increasingly sophisticated personal robots commercially available.

Article Title: Amazon Web Services CEO Matt Garman on Anthropic CEO's prediction that AI could wipe out half of all entry-level white collar jobs; says: White-collar jobs will not be wiped out because of AI, but tec
Article Text:
The Excel argument: how old technology reshaped work instead of killing it



Why Amazon is hiring 11,000 fresh grads this year



The catch: your job in two years won't look like today's



When Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei warned that AI could wipe out half of all entry-level white-collar jobs within five years, he wasn't hedging. He told Axios it could spike unemployment to 10-20% and gut hiring across tech, finance, law, and consulting. Amazon Web Services chief Matt Garman has heard the prediction. He isn't buying it.Speaking on Casey Newton's Platformer podcast, released Tuesday, June 23, Garman drew a hard line between two words. "Wipe out and change are different," he said. His argument is that white-collar jobs will be reshaped by AI, not erased by it, and he runs one of the best vantage points in the business to make that call. AWS is a roughly $150 billion-a-year operation, Anthropic's primary training partner, and the platform where millions of companies run their AI experiments. From that seat, the doomsday framing looks, to him, like bad math.Why the AWS boss thinks Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei’s "wipe out" prediction misses the pointGarman runs one of the best listening posts in the AI economy. AWS is a roughly $150 billion-a-year business, the primary training partner for Anthropic, and the platform where millions of companies run their AI experiments. From that seat, he sees the panic as overblown.His reasoning is partly economic. "If you believe half of jobs get wiped out, then the whole economy collapses on itself and everything goes away, and then you're not going to have AI, and you have to go back to those other jobs at some point. The math doesn't work out," he told Newton.Amodei's original prediction, delivered to Axios in May 2025, was blunter. The Anthropic CEO warned AI could spike unemployment to 10-20% and eliminate half of entry-level roles across tech, finance, law, and consulting, urging the industry to stop "sugar-coating" what's coming. He has since doubled down, arguing in a January 2026 essay that enduring job loss may be an "intrinsic property" of how AI replicates human cognition.Garman reached for a familiar analogy to make his case. Spreadsheet software like Microsoft Excel eliminated the jobs of people who once calculated by hand, he noted, but those workers learned to use computers and moved on. New tools spawn new roles. "The key thing is not to look at a still picture of the world and say that job's not going to exist," he said. "New jobs will be created."It's an argument with company. Nvidia's Jensen Huang and Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates have made similar cases, while Google DeepMind's Demis Hassabis talks up a future of "radical abundance." The data, so far, is muddy. A Stanford study last August found AI hitting entry-level workers hardest, particularly young software engineers. But economists like Apollo's Torsten Slok pin recent graduate struggles on broader economic forces, noting the unemployment gap for young grads opened months before ChatGPT launched.Garman's clearest evidence is a hiring number. Amazon plans to bring on 11,000 interns and new college graduates in 2026. His logic is practical, almost blunt. Entry-level workers are the cheapest to hire, haven't picked up bad habits, and arrive eager to learn new tools. "They come in with an energy and excitement, a new view on things," he said. "If you just have the exact same people you've had for the last 15 years, you don't get that energy and excitement and new ideas. "The optimism carries a condition. Garman tells Amazon employees their roles will look "vastly different" in two years, and survival depends on a willingness to learn. What he now screens for isn't a specific skill set but the ability to pick up new ones. "Do you have the willingness to dive in and learn new things?"The tension in his position is hard to miss. Amazon has cut roughly 30,000 corporate jobs since October 2025, and CEO Andy Jassy has said AI will "reduce our total corporate workforce" in the years ahead. AWS itself sells agents that recruit, code, and process insurance claims. Garman is talking up human labor even as his company ships the tools that may displace it. His answer is that Amazon has automated work for 25 years, always finding higher-value tasks for people to move into. Whether that pattern holds at AI's speed is the open question.
Article Summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei warned that AI could wipe out half of all entry-level white-collar jobs within five years. Amazon Web Services chief Matt Garman has heard the prediction and isn't buying it. His argument is that white- collar jobs will be reshaped by AI, not erased by it.

Article Title: Dell launches PowerStore Elite in India, betting big on sovereign AI storage
Article Text:
PowerStore Elite lands in India: Dell's most powerful enterprise storage platform yet, built for on-premise AI, data sovereignty and up to 5.8PB of capacity in a single 3U appliance.

Why sovereignty is the real selling point for Indian enterprises

Dell Technologies has launched PowerStore Elite in India, its most aggressive push yet to sell enterprises on running AI where their data actually lives. The storage system arrives with a pitch aimed squarely at Indian CIOs worried about data leaving the country: keep everything on-premise, keep it under your control, and still run agentic AI at production scale. It's the headline act in a wider portfolio drop carried over from Dell Technologies World 2026.The specs do the talking. PowerStore Elite packs up to 5.8 petabytes of effective capacity into a single 3U appliance, claims triple the performance and density of the previous generation, and comes with a 6:1 data reduction guarantee. Every part—drives, controllers, networking—is modular and field-upgradable, so enterprises can skip the painful storage refresh cycle and avoid downtime or data migration entirely.For regulated sectors like banking, healthcare and the public sector, the sovereignty angle isn't marketing fluff. Dell is positioning this as the foundation for agentic AI—systems that reason, plan and execute workflows on their own—without data ever leaving the organization. The new Dell AI Ecosystem folds in Google, OpenAI, NVIDIA, Hugging Face, Palantir and ServiceNow on one open on-premise platform, which is a lot of firepower to hand a company that's still stuck experimenting.There's more coming. Dell Cyber Detect, arriving for PowerStore in Q3 2026, pushes ransomware detection into the storage layer itself, inspecting data byte by byte and pinpointing the last clean copy for faster recovery. Deskside Agentic AI handles models from 30 billion to a trillion parameters locally, cutting cloud API costs by up to 87% over two years.PowerStore Elite ships in India from August 2026, with Cyber Detect following in September. Whether Indian enterprises move from AI ambition to AI outcomes, as Dell puts it, now depends on how fast they buy in.
Article Summary: Dell launches PowerStore Elite in India, its most aggressive push yet to sell enterprises on running AI where their data actually lives. The storage system arrives with a pitch aimed squarely at Indian CIOs worried about data leaving the country. The new Dell AI Ecosystem folds in Google, OpenAI, NVIDIA, Hugging Face, Palantir and ServiceNow.

Article Title: In 2014, DeepMind's DQN mastered atari games and transformed reinforcement learning
Article Text:
The Atari 2600 is a home video game console developed and produced by Atari, Inc. image credit: wikipedia

Learning through experience

The Atari 2600 Jr, a cost-reduced revision of the regular Atari 2600 video game console. Image credit: Wikimedia Commons

The ultimate test ground for digital technology

Recipe that just didn’t stop working

More than a decade ago, there was a major breakthrough in artificial intelligence in the realm of video games. In 2014, London-based DeepMind published experiments showing that an AI system could learn to play retro games such as Space Invaders and Breakout on its own, without human input. It learned to play by analyzing screen pixels and reward signals, improving through trial and error.Before this, game-playing computers were specialized and fixed, using narrow programs written by humans for a single task. DeepMind took matters into its own hands by creating a computer system capable of learning across entirely different gaming environments. The landmark editorial commentary published in Nature described this milestone as a significant breakthrough in science; it highlighted the fact that this artificial intelligence had achieved an expert level in 49 Atari games.The key to making DQN work was that it used reinforcement learning. That is to say, it had no clever hacks that would enable it to solve particular games in an elegant way. Instead, all it did was play the game and learn from its experiences to come up with the best strategy. Based on a comprehensive historical analysis published on Sensors , this revolutionary approach managed to successfully blend sophisticated neural networks with good old trial and error.It helped shift computer science research toward learning-based approaches. The focus shifted from programming a machine for one game to teaching it to learn from experience.Atari was a useful testbed because it offered dozens of visual games that used similar controls. Instead of creating a new machine for each game, scientists had one environment where they could test the algorithm on many tasks.According to an article reviewed by peers on Springer , the Arcade Learning Environment turned out to be a tremendous boost to current research efforts by creating a series of games that is still being used to evaluate the general intelligence of the machines. This was possible because game outcomes could be compared easily and the rules did not change.To prevent crashes and instability while processing images, the DeepMind researchers added mechanisms to stabilize training. One was an "experience replay" mechanism that let the system reuse past experiences, along with a target network.The technique became widely used and helped establish a standard approach for later research. Many researchers see 2014 as a turning point when deep learning and reinforcement learning came together to influence later decision-making algorithms.
Article Summary: In 2014, London-based DeepMind published experiments showing that an AI system could learn to play retro games such as Space Invaders and Breakout on its own. It learned to play by analyzing screen pixels and reward signals, improving through trial and error. The key to making DQN work was that it used reinforcement learning.

Article Title: Anthropic launches ‘Claude Science’ AI ​research workbench for scientific research
Article Text:
Unified workspace for global research



Anthropic says Claude Science is built with safety in mind



AI startup Anthropic has launched Claude Science, a specialised workbench that has been built to help scientists quickly analyse complex data, manage heavy computer workflows and accelerate breakthroughs. The new platform marks an expansion for the IPO-bound tech company into the healthcare and life sciences sector, where Google DeepMind has already got success in the form of Alphafold – an AI system that predicts a protein’s 3D structure from its amino acid sequence.Claude Science is an initiative Anthropic has been reportedly building out since October 2025. By providing an interface tailored purely for academic and medical research, the tool aims to take the mechanical tediousness out of laboratory data management.Claude Science serves as an all-in-one digital laboratory, combining coding tools, processing power and scientific databases into a single screen. According to Anthropic, the platform allows researchers to instantly review and cross-reference thousands of pages of existing medical literature.Further, it also processes raw data and instantly generate publication-ready charts and manuscript drafts. Thirdly, scientists can use the tool to map final research figures back to the exact code and laboratory environment where they were created, ensuring transparency.To ensure scientists have the right tools out of the box, Anthropic pre-configured the system with more than 60 massive scientific databases. The interface can also natively render complex, highly technical visual data, including 3D protein structures, chemistry molecular models and genome browser tracks.Anthropic says that Claude Science runs on its existing family of advanced models. The system has successfully cleared the company’s “responsible scaling” policies and specialised biosecurity evaluations to ensure the powerful technology cannot be misused.During a press briefing announcing the launch, Eric Kauderer-Abrams, Anthropic’s Head of Life Sciences, revealed that the company is also launching its own internal, pre-clinical drug discovery programs. Anthropic plans to use Claude Science to hunt for treatments for neglected diseases.
Article Summary: Anthropic has launched Claude Science, a specialised workbench. It aims to help scientists quickly analyse complex data, manage heavy computer workflows and accelerate breakthroughs. The new platform marks an expansion for the IPO-bound tech company into the healthcare and life sciences sector.

Article Title: As Elon Musk promises 'Jesus-Level' miracles from Neuralink: Here’s what he means
Article Text:
Elon Musk’s ‘Jesus-level technologies’



Restoring speech through thought



Next-generation Neuralink chip



Overshadowed by Tesla and SpaceX



Tesla CEO Elon Musk has once again raised eyebrows with bold claims about his brain-chip company Neuralink, saying its technology cloud deliver what he called ‘Jesus-level miracles’. Musk made this comment in response to a post of social media platform X (formerly known as Twitter). The post talked about Neuralink’s progress in implanting electrode threads into the brain’s protective dura mater, Musk said that the company’s technology could enable quadriplegics to walk again and the completely blind to see. He reaffirmed Neuralink’s vision of restoring lost functions and even enhancing human perception. Musk has previously touted Neuralink’s “Blindsight” chip, which he claims could restore vision and allow people to perceive multi-spectral wavelengths such as ultraviolet and infrared.For a long time, Elon Musk has touted the potential of brain implants, calling them “Jesus-level technologies”, simply for their ability to restore sight and also reverse paralysis. Elon Musk’s Neuralink promises users the ability to perform routine tasks using thought control, such as typing or moving a mouse.Earlier demonstrations showed Neuralink implants helping patients with ALS (Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis) convert thoughts into audible words, effectively restoring speech.Musk said this was part of the company’s broader mission to bridge neurological gaps with advanced brain-computer interfaces.In January, Neuralink announced its next-generation chip, expected to debut later this year. The new version will reportedly deliver three times the capabilities of current implants, expanding potential applications in both medical and lifestyle domains.Observers like Peter Hague noted that Neuralink’s breakthroughs are often overshadowed by Musk’s flashier ventures, Tesla and SpaceX. Hague argued that a mass rollout of brain-computer interfaces could have huge medical and lifestyle implications, positioning Neuralink as Musk’s most transformative project.“I think people are still not clocking what this company is going to do, because Elons other companies are much bigger and flashier. Mass rollout of brain machine interfaces is going to have huge medical implications, as well as perhaps lifestyle ones,” wrote Hague in a post on X.
Article Summary: Elon Musk has once again raised eyebrows with bold claims about his brain-chip company Neuralink. Musk said the company’s technology could enable quadriplegics to walk again and the completely blind to see. Musk made this comment in response to a post of social media platform X (formerly known as Twitter)
